signals.title: Computing with many encoded logical qubits beyond break-even

content.abstract: High-rate quantum error correcting codes encode many logical qubits in a given number of physical qubits, making them promising for quantum computation. This work demonstrates implementation of high-rate codes at scale that frustrates classical computing and improves performance with high fidelity gates and long-range qubit connectivity.

signals.title: Hybrid Consensus with Quantum Sybil Resistance

content.abstract: A consensus protocol combining classical hybrid protocols with quantum position verification for Sybil resistance. Quantum states serve as unconditionally scarce resources, providing improved energy efficiency compared to Proof-of-Work protocols.

signals.title: Self-stabilized high-dimensional quantum key distribution on a metropolitan free-space link

content.abstract: High-dimensional time-bin-encoded quantum key distribution demonstrated over hybrid link comprising 1.7 km free-space and 685 m fiber. Self-referenced architecture achieves continuous 48-hour operation using detected quantum signals exclusively.

signals.title: Discovering new photovoltaics using optimal transport theory

content.abstract: Fused Gromov-Wasserstein metric applies optimal transport theory to crystalline materials similarity assessment. Identifies seven unexplored high-efficiency photovoltaic candidates including Cs5Sb8 with predicted SLME exceeding 30%.

signals.title: Provable Last-Iterate Convergence for Multi-Objective Safe LLM Alignment via Optimistic Primal-Dual

content.abstract: Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback plays a significant role in aligning Large Language Models with human preferences. This work provides provable last-iterate convergence for multi-objective safe alignment using optimistic primal-dual methods.

signals.title: When AI Writes, Whose Voice Remains? Quantifying Cultural Marker Erasure Across World English Varieties in LLMs

content.abstract: Large Language Models are increasingly used to professionalize workplace communication, often at the cost of linguistic identity. This study quantifies how LLMs systematically erase cultural markers from World English varieties.

signals.title: GUI-Libra: Training Native GUI Agents to Reason and Act with Action-aware Supervision and Partially Verifiable RL

content.abstract: Open-source native GUI agents still lag behind closed-source systems on long-horizon navigation tasks. This work addresses the gap with action-aware supervision and partially verifiable reinforcement learning.

signals.title: The economic alignment problem of artificial intelligence

content.abstract: The alignment problem in AI research is also an economic alignment problem within a growth-based system. The paper argues that existing economic incentive structures may systematically misalign AI development with human welfare.

signals.title: The Governance of Intimacy: A Preliminary Policy Analysis of Romantic AI Platforms

content.abstract: Analysis of privacy policies in romantic AI platforms revealing data governance challenges around intimate disclosure. Examines how current regulatory frameworks fail to address the unique risks of AI companionship platforms.

signals.title: Reimagining Data Work: Participatory Annotation Workshops as Feminist Practice

content.abstract: AI systems depend on the invisible and undervalued labor of data workers, who are often treated as interchangeable units. This work reimagines annotation as participatory feminist practice.

signals.title: Runaway electron generation in ITER mitigated disruptions with improved physics models

content.abstract: Assessment of runaway-electron generation in ITER disruptions using improved physics modeling. Critical for safe operation of ITER fusion reactor, identifying conditions that may produce dangerous electron beams during plasma disruptions.

signals.title: One Brain, Omni Modalities: Towards Unified Non-Invasive Brain Decoding with Large Language Models

content.abstract: NOBEL, a neuro-omni-modal brain-encoding LLM unifying heterogeneous EEG, MEG, and fMRI signals for non-invasive brain decoding. First framework to decode brain signals into language using unified multimodal approach.

signals.title: Modelling the Index of Sustainable Economic Welfare (ISEW) and its response to policies

content.abstract: The Index of Sustainable Economic Welfare has emerged as an alternative indicator of progress in response to critiques of GDP. This work models ISEW dynamics and simulates policy responses.

signals.title: Stream Neural Networks: Epoch-Free Learning with Persistent Temporal State

content.abstract: Most contemporary neural learning systems rely on epoch-based optimization and repeated access to historical data. This work proposes stream neural networks that learn continuously without epochs, maintaining persistent temporal state.

signals.title: BEDCrypt: Privacy-preserving interval analytics with homomorphic encryption for genomics

content.abstract: Privacy-preserving system for genomic interval analytics based on homomorphic encryption in an honest-but-curious server setting. Enables secure computation over sensitive genomic data without revealing individual sequences.

signals.title: SWE-Protege: Learning to Selectively Collaborate With an Expert Unlocks Small LMs as Software Engineering Agents

content.abstract: Small language models offer cost, latency, and adaptability advantages but lag on software engineering tasks. SWE-Protege learns when to delegate to expert models, achieving near-expert performance at fraction of cost.

signals.title: Are Foundation Models the Route to Full-Stack Transfer in Robotics?

content.abstract: Overview of foundation models' impact on transfer learning across all abstraction levels in robotics. Examines whether pre-trained vision-language-action models can enable truly general-purpose robotic systems.

signals.title: DySCO: Dynamic Attention-Scaling Decoding for Long-Context LMs

content.abstract: Understanding and reasoning over long contexts is crucial for language models. DySCO proposes dynamic attention scaling to improve long-context reasoning without retraining.

signals.title: Generalisation of RLHF under Reward Shift and Clipped KL Regularisation

content.abstract: Alignment and adaptation in LLMs heavily rely on RLHF. This work analyzes generalization under reward distribution shift and proposes clipped KL regularization for robustness.

signals.title: Hierarchical LLM-Based Multi-Agent Framework with Prompt Optimization for Multi-Robot Task Planning

content.abstract: Multi-robot task planning requires decomposing natural-language instructions into executable actions. This hierarchical LLM framework optimizes prompts for coordinated multi-robot execution.

signals.title: Enabling End-to-End APT Emulation in Industrial Environments: SIMPLE-ICS Testbed

content.abstract: Virtualised industrial testbed enabling multi-stage APT campaign emulation across IT, OT, and IIoT environments. Critical for testing cybersecurity defenses in industrial control systems.

signals.title: Academic collaborations and movements towards successful careers in physics

content.abstract: Analysing 35,708 physicists' careers spanning 15+ years reveals ten distinct evolution patterns of network size. Identifies collaboration patterns predictive of career success and mobility.

signals.title: Can ranked-choice voting elect the least popular candidate?

content.abstract: Analysis of how frequently instant runoff voting selects the weakest candidate in three-candidate elections. Provides mathematical bounds on pathological outcomes in electoral systems.

signals.title: Extending Sequence Length is Not All You Need: Multimodal Signals for Gene Expression Prediction

content.abstract: Current models show long sequence modeling can decrease performance for gene expression prediction; proximal multimodal epigenomic signals prove essential for accurate predictions.

signals.title: Phase-Dependent Excitonic Light Harvesting and Photovoltaic Limits in Monolayer Y2TeO2 MOenes

content.abstract: Phase-dependent electronic and excitonic phenomena in monolayer Y2TeO2 with direct gaps in near-infrared to visible range. Exciton binding energies 126-152 meV suggest promising new class of 2D photovoltaic materials.

signals.title: Secure Semantic Communications via AI Defenses: Fundamentals, Solutions, and Future Directions

content.abstract: Comprehensive survey on security in semantic communications via AI defense, analyzing threat models and defense strategies for next-generation communication systems.

signals.title: Off-The-Shelf Image-to-Image Models Are All You Need To Defeat Image Protection Schemes

content.abstract: Advances in Generative AI have led to development of protection strategies to prevent unauthorized use of images. This work demonstrates that generic image-to-image models can circumvent all major protection schemes.

signals.title: Loss Mechanisms in High-coherence Multimode Mechanical Resonators Coupled to Superconducting Circuits

content.abstract: Circuit quantum acoustodynamics devices demonstrate phonon lifetimes up to 400 microseconds with coherence times approaching one millisecond. Critical for quantum memory and quantum networking.

signals.title: Modeling plant disease spread via high-resolution human mobility networks

content.abstract: Integrates a high-resolution network of human movements with a metapopulation model to simulate pathogen transmission dynamics in agricultural systems.

signals.title: Noise-adaptive hybrid quantum convolutional neural networks based on depth-stratified feature extraction

content.abstract: Hierarchical quantum classifiers adapting to noise levels through depth-stratified feature extraction. Demonstrates practical quantum advantage in noisy intermediate-scale quantum devices.
